# N# top 25 diagnostic golden suite
# Stable terminal rendering from OutputFormatter.DiagnosticsToText.
# Docs URLs are category-qualified because some diagnostic codes overlap across parser/analyzer/migration domains.

Diagnostic clusters (25 groups, 25 diagnostics)

       recipe: manual-triage:inspect-root-diagnostic

       next command: nlc query inspect --file analyzer/cannot-infer.nl --pos 2:5
       example: [analyzer] Cannot infer type
       next: Start at the root example and decide whether this is a converter, refactor, or compiler diagnostic issue.
       next: After fixing the root cause, rerun diagnostics and compare the remaining cluster counts.

       next command: nlc query inspect --file analyzer/missing-return.nl --pos 1:1

       next: Start at the root example and decide whether this is a converter, refactor, or compiler diagnostic issue.
       next: After fixing the root cause, rerun diagnostics and compare the remaining cluster counts.
  [1x] diagnostic-message-shape / control-flow / risk: low
       recipe: manual-triage:inspect-root-diagnostic

       next command: nlc query inspect --file analyzer/non-exhaustive-match.nl --pos 3:12
       example: [analyzer] Pattern matching is not exhaustive
       next: Start at the root example and decide whether this is a converter, refactor, or compiler diagnostic issue.
       next: After fixing the root cause, rerun diagnostics and compare the remaining cluster counts.
  [1x] type-mismatch / variable-declaration / risk: medium
       recipe: refactor:signature-or-expression-shape
       root: analyzer/type-mismatch.nl:3:19
       next command: nlc query inspect --file analyzer/type-mismatch.nl --pos 3:19
       example: [analyzer] Type mismatch
       next: Compare the expected and actual types at the root example and update the refactor recipe that changed the expression/signature shape.
       next: Prefer fixing the producer expression over adding casts to each cascaded consumer.
  [1x] member-resolution / return-statement / risk: medium
       recipe: migration:api-rename-or-extension-import
       root: analyzer/undefined-member.nl:4:17
       next command: nlc query inspect --file analyzer/undefined-member.nl --pos 4:17
       example: [analyzer] Member 'Lenght' not found on type 'string'
       next: Verify the API/member rename map for the root receiver before fixing repeated call sites.
       next: Check whether an extension-method import or receiver type conversion was dropped.
  [1x] type-resolution / variable-declaration / risk: medium
       recipe: migration:type-map-or-import-resolution
       root: analyzer/undefined-type.nl:1:12
       next command: nlc query inspect --file analyzer/undefined-type.nl --pos 1:12
       example: [analyzer] Type 'Usr' not found
       next: Patch the type mapping/import recipe at the earliest root location before chasing downstream uses.
       next: Check whether the source construct needs full qualification or a project reference.
  [1x] identifier-resolution / return-statement / risk: medium
       recipe: migration:missing-import-qualification-or-rename
       root: analyzer/undefined-variable.nl:6:12
       next command: nlc query inspect --file analyzer/undefined-variable.nl --pos 6:12
       example: [analyzer] Variable 'totla' not found
       next: Resolve the first missing identifier by adding the import/qualification or updating the rename map.
       next: Rerun diagnostics after the root symbol is resolved; dependent member/type errors may disappear.
  [1x] diagnostic-message-shape / call-or-construction / risk: low
       recipe: manual-triage:inspect-root-diagnostic
       root: analyzer/wrong-argument-count.nl:5:5
       next command: nlc query inspect --file analyzer/wrong-argument-count.nl --pos 5:5
       example: [analyzer] Function 'send' expects 2 arguments but got 1
       next: Start at the root example and decide whether this is a converter, refactor, or compiler diagnostic issue.
       next: After fixing the root cause, rerun diagnostics and compare the remaining cluster counts.
  [1x] syntax-missing-delimiter / call-or-construction / risk: high
       recipe: migration:delimiter-balancing
       root: parser/missing-argument.nl:3:18
       next command: nlc query inspect --file parser/missing-argument.nl --pos 3:18
       example: [parser] Unexpected token ')'
       next: Fix the earliest statement-boundary parse error first; later syntax diagnostics are often cascades.
       next: Inspect the migration/refactor recipe that emitted this construct and add a delimiter/terminator regression test.
  [1x] syntax-missing-delimiter / variable-declaration / risk: high
       recipe: migration:delimiter-balancing
       root: parser/missing-call-paren.nl:4:22
       next command: nlc query inspect --file parser/missing-call-paren.nl --pos 4:22
       example: [parser] Missing closing parenthesis
       next: Fix the earliest statement-boundary parse error first; later syntax diagnostics are often cascades.
       next: Inspect the migration/refactor recipe that emitted this construct and add a delimiter/terminator regression test.
  ... 15 more clusters omitted; use --json for the full AI-consumable cluster list.

── [NL101] ERROR ──────────── parser/missing-argument.nl:3:18 ──

    3 | print(, name)
      |                  ^

[parser] Unexpected token ')'

The parser found `)` while it was still looking for an expression argument.

Suggestion: Add the missing expression before `)` or remove the dangling comma.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/parser/NL101

── [NL102] ERROR ───── parser/missing-parameter-colon.nl:1:12 ──

    1 | func greet(name string) {
      |            ^

[parser] Expected ':' after parameter name

Function parameters use `name: Type`; without the colon, the type name is parsed in the wrong slot.

Suggestion: Write `func greet(name: string) { ... }`.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/parser/NL102

── [NL103] ERROR ─── parser/object-initializer-equals.nl:2:28 ──

    2 |     return new User { Name = "Ada" }
      |                            ^

[parser] Invalid syntax in object initializer

N# object initializers use colon fields; `=` is a C# migration leftover here.

Suggestion: Use `new User { Name: "Ada" }`.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/parser/NL103

── [NL104] ERROR ──────── parser/missing-closing-brace.nl:5:1 ──


[parser] Unexpected end of file

The file ended before the parser found the closing `}` for the current block.

Suggestion: Add the missing closing brace and re-run `nlc check`.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/parser/NL104

── [NL106] ERROR ────────── parser/missing-match-brace.nl:7:1 ──

    7 | match status {
      | ^

[parser] Missing closing brace

A block started here but never closed, so later code may be attached to the wrong scope.

Suggestion: Close the block with `}` at the indentation level where the construct began.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/parser/NL106

── [NL107] ERROR ────────── parser/missing-call-paren.nl:4:22 ──

    4 |     total := add(first, second
      |                      ^

[parser] Missing closing parenthesis

This call opened `(` but did not close it before the line ended.

Suggestion: Add `)` after the final argument: `add(first, second)`.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/parser/NL107

── [NL202] ERROR ───────────── analyzer/type-mismatch.nl:3:19 ──

    3 | let count: int = "five"
      |                   ^^^^^

[analyzer] Type mismatch

This expression produces `string`, but the annotation says `int`.

Suggestion: Parse the string intentionally or change the annotation to `string`.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/analyzer/NL202

── [NL203] ERROR ─────────────── analyzer/cannot-infer.nl:2:5 ──

    2 | let value = null
      |     ^^^^^

[analyzer] Cannot infer type

`null` by itself does not tell the analyzer which nullable type you want.

Suggestion: Add an explicit type, for example `let value: string? = null`.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/analyzer/NL203

── [NL301] ERROR ──────── analyzer/undefined-variable.nl:6:12 ──

    6 |     return totla
      |            ^^^^^

[analyzer] Variable 'totla' not found

There is no local, parameter, or member named `totla` in scope.

Suggestion: Did you mean `total`? Fix the spelling or declare the variable before use.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/analyzer/NL301

── [NL302] ERROR ──────────── analyzer/undefined-type.nl:1:12 ──

    1 | let user: Usr
      |            ^^^

[analyzer] Type 'Usr' not found

The analyzer cannot resolve `Usr` from this file's declarations or imports.

Suggestion: Import the type, define it, or correct the spelling to `User`.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/analyzer/NL302

── [NL303] ERROR ────────── analyzer/undefined-member.nl:4:17 ──

    4 |     return name.Lenght
      |                 ^^^^^^

[analyzer] Member 'Lenght' not found on type 'string'

The receiver type is `string`, and `Lenght` is not one of its members.

Suggestion: Did you mean `Length`? Use the exact member name exposed by the type.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/analyzer/NL303

── [NL305] ERROR ───────────── analyzer/missing-return.nl:1:1 ──

    1 | func score(ok: bool): int {
      | ^^^^

[analyzer] Not all code paths return a value of type 'int'

This function promises to return `int`, but at least one branch can fall off the end.

Suggestion: Return an `int` on every path, or change the return type to `void` if no value is needed.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/analyzer/NL305

── [NL401] ERROR ─────── analyzer/wrong-argument-count.nl:5:5 ──

    5 | send(email)
      |     ^^^^^^^^^

[analyzer] Function 'send' expects 2 arguments but got 1

The call is missing one required argument from the function signature.

Suggestion: Pass the missing value, or update the function signature if it should be optional.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/analyzer/NL401

── [NL501] ERROR ────── analyzer/non-exhaustive-match.nl:3:12 ──

    3 |     match color {
      |            ^^^^^

[analyzer] Pattern matching is not exhaustive

The match does not handle every possible value of `Color`.

Suggestion: Add arms for the missing cases or a final `_ => ...` arm when a catch-all is intentional.

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/analyzer/NL501

── [NL101] INFO ─────────────── Services/Users/UserDto.nl:6:1 ──

    6 | public partial class UserDto {
      | ^^^^^^

[migration] C# modifier 'public' looks out of place in an N# file

C# modifiers leak source-language visibility rules into N#; N# uses naming/export conventions instead.

Suggestion: Remove the C# modifier and use N# naming/casing/export conventions instead

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/migration/NL101

── [NL102] INFO ────────────── Services/Users/UserDto.nl:4:13 ──

    4 |     Id: int { get; set; }
      |             ^^^^^^^^^^^^^

[migration] C# auto-property accessor block '{ get; set; }' should be converted to N# property/record syntax

Auto-property accessor blocks are C# syntax, not the N# property or record shape.

Suggestion: For DTO-shaped data, prefer an N# record; otherwise write explicit N# property syntax

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/migration/NL102

── [NL103] INFO ────────────── Services/Users/UserDto.nl:8:20 ──

    8 |     Name: string = default!
      |                    ^^^^^^^^

[migration] Null-forgiving artifact 'default!' is a C# migration leftover

Null-forgiving syntax hides a nullable design decision that must be made explicitly during migration.

Suggestion: Replace the placeholder with a real initializer, explicit nullable/default value, or a reviewed construction path; do not carry C# default-forgiving syntax into N#

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/migration/NL103

── [NL104] INFO ───────────── Services/Users/UserDto.nl:10:18 ──

    10 |         if users.TryGetValue(id, out var user) {
       |                  ^^^^^^^^^^^

[migration] C# out parameter / TryGetValue pattern is a migration candidate

`out` parameters and `TryGetValue` patterns are migration smells because N# prefers values, tuples, and result-style APIs.

Suggestion: Prefer an N# tuple/result-returning helper or a pattern that avoids out parameters

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/migration/NL104

── [NL105] INFO ─────────────── Services/Users/UserDto.nl:3:7 ──

    3 | class OrderDto {
      |       ^^^^^^^^

[migration] DTO-shaped class 'OrderDto' looks like an N# record candidate

This data-only DTO shape is probably a record in idiomatic N# unless identity or inheritance matters.

Suggestion: Convert data-only DTO classes to records when identity/inheritance semantics are not required

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/migration/NL105

── [NL106] INFO ───────────── Services/Users/UserDto.nl:19:11 ──

    19 |         } catch (ex) {
       |           ^^^^^

[migration] try/catch returning HTTP 500 boilerplate is a migration candidate

Repeated catch-to-HTTP-500 blocks obscure intent and should move to centralized error handling or result mapping.

Suggestion: Prefer centralized error handling or an N# result/error abstraction instead of repeated catch-to-500 blocks

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/migration/NL106

── [NL107] INFO ─────────────── Services/Users/UserDto.nl:1:1 ──

    1 | using System;
      | ^^^^^

[migration] C# using directive is a migration blocker in an N# file

C# `using` directives must become N# imports or project references before this file is considered migrated.

Suggestion: Convert C# using directives to N# import declarations or project references

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/migration/NL107

── [NL108] INFO ─────────────── Services/Users/UserDto.nl:2:1 ──

    2 | namespace Legacy.Api;
      | ^^^^^^^^^

[migration] C# namespace declaration is a migration blocker in an N# file

C# namespace declarations fight N# package layout and should be expressed with `package`.

Suggestion: Use an N# package declaration such as `package My.Package` instead of `namespace My.Package;`

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/migration/NL108

── [NL109] INFO ─────────────── Services/Users/UserDto.nl:1:1 ──

    1 | using System;
      | ^^^^^^^^

[migration] N# file is missing package declaration `package Services.Users` for its package layout

The file layout implies a package, but the source does not declare the matching N# package.

Suggestion: Add `package Services.Users` as the first declaration in this .nl file

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/migration/NL109

── [NL110] INFO ───────────── Services/Users/UserDto.nl:15:39 ──

    15 |     func Create(): User => new User { Name = "Ada" }
       |                                       ^^^^

[migration] C# equals-style object initializer is a migration blocker in an N# file

Equals-style object initializer members are valid C#, but canonical N# initializer members use `:`.

Suggestion: Use canonical N# object initialization with colon fields: `new Type { Name: value }`

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/migration/NL110

── [NL111] INFO ───────────── Services/Users/UserDto.nl:13:22 ──

    13 |         return result.Value
       |                      ^^^^^^

[migration] Unsafe '.Value' access 'result.Value' is a migration leftover that can throw when the value is absent

Direct `.Value` unwraps can throw; migration needs an explicit absence-handling path.

Suggestion: Prefer `match`, `??`, or a direct `x != null` guard before unwrapping; only keep `.Value` when the wrapper type owns that API and the access is proven safe

See: https://docs.n-sharp.dev/errors/migration/NL111


Found 14 errors, 11 info.
